name: Jalapeno Cornbread
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Prepare a 9-inch square baking pan with cooking spray and flour.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine dry ingredients: cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and baking soda.
4. In another mixing bowl, whip the egg whites until foamy.
5. Add the remaining ingredients (buttermilk, onions, corn, green peppers, and jalapeno peppers) to the egg white mixture and blend well.
6. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just moistened.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared baking pan.
8. Bake for 30-35 minutes or until golden brown. Cool slightly and serve immediately.
Notes:
Buttermilk substitute: combine 1 1/2 teaspoons vinegar with enough milk to equal 1/2 cup.
Baking breads at a lower temperature results in thicker, chewier crusts; baking at a higher temperature results in thinner, crisper crusts.
